Rugby.title: Saracens' giant Totoa Auva'a off the hook for nightclub brawl
Rugby.summary: Saracens lock Totoa Auvaa, who was at the centre of a bust-up with England fast bowler Gus Atkinson, has been told that he will face no disciplinary action for his Chelsea nightclub scrap.
Rugby.body:
Saracens lock Totoa Auva’a, who was at the centre of a bust-up with England fast bowler Gus Atkinson, has been told that he will face no disciplinary action for his Chelsea nightclub scrap.

Highly rated Samoan-born prospect Auva’a, 21, who plays lock or blindside flanker, attempted to punch Atkinson while the England cricket team were out celebrating a win over New Zealand, but missed and hit an England and Wales Cricket Board security guard instead.

Rugby.title: 'It's a shame': Wales camp react as Christ Tshiunza 'deemed unavailable'
Rugby.summary: Wales boss Steve Tandy admits it is a “shame” new Sale signing Christ Tshiunza will be unavailable because of the Welsh Rugby Union’s 25-cap ruling.
Rugby.body:
Wales boss Steve Tandy admits it is a “shame” new Sale signing Christ Tshiunza will be unavailable because of the Welsh Rugby Union’s 25-cap ruling. The 24-year-old flanker will end his five-year stay at Exeter – where he began his Test career and the ruling did not affect him as an uncapped player – this summer and head north to Manchester, despite having had interest from Welsh sides Cardiff and Ospreys.

The WRU selection policy requires players that represent clubs outside Wales to have won at least 25 caps, but Tshiunza is on 15 and will be unable to play international rugby next season in the build-up to the 2027 World Cup.

Rugby.title: Sleepless nights and inner torment: Marcus Smith opens up on England career
Rugby.summary: The Harlequins playmaker remains part of Steve Borthwick’s squad for next month’s Nations Championship matches.
Rugby.body:
Marcus Smith will continue riding his England roller coaster as he reflects with pride on a career that has caused sleepless nights spent ruminating on mistakes. Smith won his 50th cap in the final match of the Six Nations against France and remains part of Steve Borthwick’s squad for next month’s Nations Championship matches against South Africa, Fiji and Argentina.

Seemingly destined from 2022 to act as England’s fly-half for the foreseeable future, the electric Harlequins playmaker was instead converted to full-back and has since been remoulded as a bench option who covers both positions.

Rugby.summary: The Nations Championship kicks off next week, and between now and then, Wallabies players will have their final opportunities to impress coach Joe Schmidt for matchday selection.
Rugby.body:
The Nations Championship kicks off next week, and between now and then, Wallabies players will have their final opportunities to impress coach Joe Schmidt for matchday selection.

A hallmark of the first Wallabies squad of 2026 was that it was mostly picked on red-hot Super Rugby Pacific or other club form, and Queensland Reds fullback Jock Campbell is no exception to that criterion.

Rugby.title: Handful of new faces selected in Australia U20 team to play Spain
Rugby.summary: There are some new faces in the Australia U20 team named to play Spain in their opening match of the World Rugby Junior World Championship, headlined by rising Queensland Reds star Treyvon Pritchard.
Rugby.body:
There are some new faces in the Australia U20 team named to play Spain in their opening match of the World Rugby Junior World Championship, headlined by rising Queensland Reds star Treyvon Pritchard.

The 12-cap Reds flyer has been named in the No.12 jersey, with France-based pair Lehopoame Leota and Kingbenjamin Swerling-Finaipepe also in line for potential U20 debuts off the bench.

Rugby.title: Scarlets sign New Zealand forward Grace
Rugby.summary: Once-capped New Zealand forward Cullen Grace will join Scarlets from Super Rugby side Crusaders in October.
Rugby.body:
Scarlets have signed New Zealand back-rower Cullen Grace from Super Rugby side Crusaders. The 26-year-old has made one Test appearance for the All Blacks, against Australia in 2020, while he has also represented the Maori All Blacks on three occasions.

Grace will miss the start of the 2026-27 United Rugby Championship (URC) season and link up with Scarlets in October, following the completion of Canterbury's National Provincial Championship campaign later this year.

Rugby.title: Saracens' Auvaa not punished for Stokes incident
Rugby.summary: Saracens forward Totoa Auvaa is not sanctioned after he was involved in a nightclub incident with England cricket captain Ben Stokes.
Rugby.body:
Saracens forward Totoa Auvaa will not be sanctioned after he was involved in a nightclub incident with England cricket captain Ben Stokes and bowler Gus Atkinson. Stokes and Atkinson were made unavailable for England's second Test defeat by New Zealand pending an inquiry into events that unfolded when they were out celebrating victory in the first Test.

Saracens have carried out their own investigation and are standing by academy player Auvaa, 21, while criticising the England and Wales Cricket Board (ECB) for not involving them in the process.

Saracens have carried out their own investigation and are standing by academy player Auvaa, 21, while criticising the England and Wales Cricket Board (ECB) for not involving them in the process.

Rugby.title: Breanne Nicholas still kicking with Canada’s rugby sevens team
Rugby.summary: Breanne Nicholas still kicking with Canada’s rugby sevens team Chatham Daily News
Rugby.body:
Breanne Nicholas prefers to avoid the limelight, but that was impossible this season. The Blenheim veteran became the most-capped player in the history of Canada’s women’s rugby sevens team.

“I wasn’t keeping track of that,” said Nicholas, 32. “My coaches kept reminding me or putting it to the forefront. They definitely celebrated it a lot. “It is a big thing. I know I am one to diminish accomplishments and not make a big deal of it, but it is something to be the most-capped Canadian for sevens for the women.”

With the Gibraltar 7s kicking off this Thursday evening at the Europa Sports Complex, Gibraltar will see a full schedule of international sport across its main venues throughout the weekend.

Rugby.summary: Former South Africa loosehead prop Steven Kitshoff has highlighted the importance of Felix Jones ahead of the Springboks' Nations Championship meeting with England at Ellis Park next week.
Rugby.body:
Former South Africa loosehead prop Steven Kitshoff has highlighted the importance of Felix Jones ahead of the Springboks’ Nations Championship meeting with England at Ellis Park next week.

The former Ireland international, who was part of Rassie Erasmus’ backroom staff for their two World Cup triumphs, rejoined the Springbok set-up last year after a year working under Steve Borthwick with England.

Rugby.title: Wood to officiate PWR Final 86 days after giving birth to second child
Rugby.summary: Holly Wood will referee Sunday's 2026 Premiership Women's Rugby Final just 86 after she gave birth to her second child.
Rugby.body:
Holly Wood will referee Sunday’s 2026 Premiership Women’s Rugby Final between Saracens and Trailfinders Women just 86 after she gave birth to her second child. The former Harlequins and Richmond wing, who took up refereeing at the conclusion of her playing career, gave birth to her first child, Huxley, in 2023 and welcomed her daughter, Adaline, into the world in April.

In an interview with PWR, Wood revealed that she learned of her second pregnancy as she prepared to officiate at Women’s Rugby World Cup 2025. Wood opted to keep the pregnancy a secret during the tournament, was a lead official until the 20th week of her pregnancy and was an assistant until the end of her second trimester.

Rugby.title: French heatwave could see Top 14 Final played at record temperatures
Rugby.summary: The Top 14 Final in Paris could be played at record-breaking temperatures this Saturday night as France continues to be gripped by a severe heatwave.
Rugby.body:
The Top 14 Final in Paris could be played at record-breaking temperatures this Saturday night as France continues to be gripped by a severe heatwave. Despite the game kicking off at 9 pm local time, Montpellier and Toulouse could be playing in soaring temperatures, with it forecast to be as hot as 34 degrees at kick-off in Saint-Denis at the Stade de France.

The conditions are similar to the scenes seen in Bilbao during the EPCR Challenge Cup and Investec Champions Cup final last month, where matches kicked off at 31 and 34 degrees.

Rugby.title: Munster duo John Ryan & Niall Scannell: ‘We’ve left those jerseys in a good place’
Rugby.summary: The Ireland front-rowers recall 'surviving the slagging' at Munster, a Rassie rollocking and the highs and lows of their last 13 years together.
Rugby.body:
“You think you’re jumping on to have a nostalgic talk about how much you love the club and all your team-mates, and all that. Then the first thing you get is all your team-mates have actually ratted you out and told a load of stories about you!”

It is Monday after the ‘third or fourth goodbye’ and it is now official – Niall Scannell and John Ryan are former professional rugby players. Both are not long back from school runs and have items on the to-do lists that need ticking off. Are they able to give up an hour of their mornings to look back on their rugby careers? “I could sit here all morning,” Scannell jokes. “Take all the time you need.”

Rugby.title: AXA Élite 1 Final preview: Not the finale we expected, but the one we deserve
Rugby.summary: Sunday’s AXA Elite 1 final in Biarritz between ASM Romagnat and Stade Bordelais may not be the match-up many had expected, but it is the final the French domestic league deserves.
Rugby.body:
Sunday’s AXA Elite 1 final in Biarritz between ASM Romagnat and Stade Bordelais – a repeat of the 2024 showpiece match – may not be the match-up many had expected, but it is the final the French domestic league deserves.

The good news is that it should be much cooler on the South West coast of France at kick-off than it has been all week, as the heatwave that has blasted the country this week finally relents.

Rugby.title: 'When he stood up in a meeting, the coaches would sit down... we didn't even need a coach'
Rugby.summary: Former South Africa utility back Francois Hougaard has described his former Springboks and Bulls team-mate Fourie du Preez as a "genius", revealing that the legendary scrum-half was effectively a coach on the field during their time together at the Bulls.
Rugby.body:
'When he stood up in a meeting, the coaches would sit down... we didn't even need a coach' Former South Africa utility back Francois Hougaard has described his former Springboks and Bulls team-mate Fourie du Preez as a “genius”, revealing that the legendary scrum-half was effectively a coach on the field during their time together at the Bulls.

The 38-year-old joined the Pretoria franchise after starring for South Africa at age-grade level, despite being warned that his route to the first team would be blocked by Du Preez and fellow scrum-half Heini Adams.

Rugby.title: WXV Global Series: Italy out for World Cup revenge on home soil
Rugby.summary: Italy will be out for Women's Rugby World Cup revenge against South Africa when they host the southern hemisphere side in the WXV Global Series in September.
Rugby.body:
Italy will be out for Women’s Rugby World Cup revenge against South Africa when they host the southern hemisphere side in the WXV Global Series in September. The Italian Rugby Federation have confirmed that their women’s national team will host Japan and South Africa in their home WXV Global Series fixtures this September.

They will host the fixtures across back-to-back weekends in September at Stadio Omero Tognon in Fontanafredda. Italy have played Japan eight times in the two teams’ history. The Europeans have won six of those fixtures, drawn one and lost once.

Rugby.title: Newcastle Red Bulls swoop for ex-Blitz Bok Werner Kok
Rugby.summary: Ulster winger Werner Kok has signed a one-year deal with Newcastle Red Bulls, which could be a sign that the Gallagher PREM outfit are losing their battle to bring Fehi Fineanganofo to Kingston Park next season.
Rugby.body:
Ulster winger Werner Kok has signed a one-year deal with Newcastle Red Bulls, which could be a sign that the Gallagher PREM outfit are losing their battle to bring Fehi Fineanganofo to Kingston Park next season.

Hurricanes speedster Fineanganofo, who broke the Rugby Pacific try-scoring record, is keen to rip up his two-year Red Bulls contract after being named as one of four uncapped players included in Dave Rennie’s first All Blacks squad of the year earlier this week.

Rugby.title: Trailfinders target 'underdog' victory against Saracens
Rugby.summary: Ealing Trailfinders look for a first-ever Premiership Women's Rugby title as they face three-time winners Saracens at the Stoop.
Rugby.body:
Trailfinders' England captain Meg Jones says they are targeting an "underdog victory" in their first Premiership Women's Rugby (PWR) final. The West Londoners only joined the PWR in 2023 and had never finished higher than sixth. They secured fourth spot before beating triple champions Gloucester-Hartpury 29-26 at Kingsholm in their semi-final on June 14.

Trailfinders now face three-time champions Saracens at the Stoop this Sunday, with the North London outfit chasing a record fourth title. "I honestly didn't think I'd be here," Jones told BBC Sport. "It would be an underdog story and mean a huge amount to the club. And on the back of the season we've had, it would mean a great deal to me as well."

Rugby.title: Carys Phillips creates unique piece of Barbarians history with Allianz Stadium selection
Rugby.summary: Carys Phillips' selection for the Barbarians this weekend has seen her make a unique piece of history for the world famous invitational side.
Rugby.body:
Carys Phillips‘ selection for the Barbarians this weekend has seen her make a unique piece of history for the world famous invitational side. The 87-cap hooker has followed her father, Rowland, in gaining selection for the side. They are the first father-daughter team to represent the Barbarians.

Phillips, 33, was previously coached by her father for Wales and captained the team during that time. Rowland Phillips is currently the head coach of Wales Deaf Men’s team. Earlier this year, Phillips gained selection to Sean Lynn’s Wales squad for the Guinness Women’s Six Nations, but did not gain selection for any of the team’s five defeats in the tournament. Her last cap came in Wales’ Women’s Rugby World Cup 2025 loss to Fiji.

Rugby.title: Why Alisha and Jasmine Joyce wanted to play domestic rugby in Wales next season
Rugby.summary: In choosing to leave Bristol Bears and return to Wales, Jasmine and Alisha Joyce made a clear decision: family over career. At least in the short term.
Rugby.body:
In choosing to leave Bristol Bears and return to Wales, Jasmine and Alisha Joyce made a clear decision: family over career. At least in the short term. Not in the sense that returning to play on the ‘right’ side of the Severn dramatically hampers their rugby. They will line up in the Celtic Challenge. Both have already done so for the Brython Thunder. International duty, of course, is still in the picture.

The Joyces live in west Wales. In choosing to forego lengthy commutes to Bristol, they will be staying closer to family support now that their young son, Ralphie, has come along. They have chosen family over access to Premiership Women’s Rugby.

Rugby.title: Argubly England's 2 best back-rows to lock horns in Top 14 Final
Rugby.summary: Stade Toulousain and Montpellier HR are just a few days away from finding out which of the two will take the Top 14 trophy home, with several UK players set to be involved in what’s expected to be a game for the ages.
Rugby.body:
Stade Toulousain and Montpellier HR are just a few days away from finding out which of the two will take the Top 14 trophy home, with several UK players set to be involved in what’s expected to be a game for the ages.

With Jack Willis and Blair Kinghorn ready to help Ugo Mola’s Toulouse reach a 25th Bouclier de Brennus – in what would be their 4th consecutive title – Montpellier are bringing Adam Beard, Ali Price and Billy Vunipola to the Stade de France, hellbent on putting an end to Les Rouge et Noir’s reign.

Rugby.title: PWR secures first multi-million pound sponsor deal
Rugby.summary: Premiership Women's Rugby (PWR) has secured its first title sponsorship deal which will see over £3m invested into the league.
Rugby.body:
Premiership Women's Rugby (PWR) has secured its first paying title sponsor which will see more than £3m invested into the league. The contract with car company Jaecoo will see more than £1m put into the league for each season of the three-year deal.

As part of the deal, from the 2026/27 season the league will be known as the Jaecoo PWR. Speaking to BBC Sport, PWR executive chair Genevieve Shore said the lack of a previous title sponsor investing into the league had been "hanging over PWR" and described the new deal as "momentous and joyful".

Rugby.title: Four uncapped players receive Wales call to face the Barbarians
Rugby.summary: Four players are in line to make their Wales debuts after Steve Tandy named his side to face the Barbarians at Twickenham's Allianz Stadium on Saturday.
Rugby.body:
Four players are in line to make their Wales debuts after Steve Tandy named his side to face the Barbarians at Twickenham’s Allianz Stadium on Saturday. Cardiff loosehead Rhys Barratt, Ospreys tighthead Ben Warren, Dragons lock Ryan Woodman and Dragons back-row Harrison Keddie have all been included among the replacements for the uncapped fixture.

The quartet headline a Wales squad who also welcome back second-row Teddy Williams following injury, while Dewi Lake captains a side packed with established internationals as preparations begin for the Nations Championship.

Rugby.title: Former Bok hopeful Darrien Landsberg to join Exeter to kickstart Test career
Rugby.summary: Exeter Chiefs are poised to signal Black Knight Entertainment’s takeover at Sandy Park by announcing the arrival of Zimbabwe-born lock Darrien Landsberg from United Rugby Championship side the Lions.
Rugby.body:
Exeter Chiefs are poised to signal Black Knight Entertainment’s takeover at Sandy Park by announcing the arrival of Zimbabwe-born lock Darrien Landsberg from United Rugby Championship side the Lions.

Landsberg, who will be 28 in July, was knocking on Rassie Erasmus’ door last year for an invitation to the Springbok alignment camp taking place in Cape Town, but missed out and has now decided it’s time to head over to Europe.

Rugby.title: 'Almost unbelievable': The England player Borthwick must now back
Rugby.summary: Steve Borthwick must now finally follow the PREM champions’ example this summer, starting with backing Fin Smith to the hilt.
Rugby.body:
Steve Borthwick must now finally follow the PREM champions’ example this summer, starting with backing Fin Smith to the hilt. It’s almost unbelievable that he has just seven Test starts to his name when he is a British & Irish Lion and has led his team to the league title in two of the last three seasons but there is no way he can be overlooked again.

The Northampton man was parachuted into an already struggling side for the final two defeats of a disastrous 2026 Six Nations campaign, and only started once against Fiji in the autumn, that after winning all four games he started in the previous Six Nations.

Rugby.title: Callender to skipper inexperienced Wales team against Barbarians in Twickenham
Rugby.summary: Alex Callender will captain an inexperienced Wales team against the Barbarians on Saturday afternoon at Allianz Stadium.
Rugby.body:
Alex Callender will captain an inexperienced Wales team against the Barbarians on Saturday afternoon at Allianz Stadium. The squad selected by Sean Lynn contains five players without Test match honours for the uncapped fixture. Callender returns to the team after she missed the entirety of the Guinness Women’s Six Nations with injury.

Wales’ fixture against the Barbarians forms part of a doubleheader in West London, with Wales’ men taking on the Barbarians earlier in the afternoon. Two uncapped players are in the starting XV, with wings Amy Williams and Kelsie Webster getting their first taste of international rugby. Nel Metcalfe, who also missed the Women’s Six Nations, completes the back three at full-back.

Rugby.title: The best rugby commentators and pundits of the season
Rugby.summary: The art of commentating and rugby punditry isn't easily learnt but this list of rugby luminaries are the best in the business
Rugby.body:
Rugby players always get the plaudits, but where’s the love for the pundits? For a bit of fun, here are my list of top pundits and commentators from the 2025/26 season – not in any particular order, just some of the best to take the mic*.

Very few players dominate their rugby playing career, retire, and then instantly dominate the comms and punditry world. Warburton did just that. His approach to commentary is exactly as he played – heavy on detail and executed in a way that fans understand. Most players have in-depth knowledge of their position, and the game as a whole, but very few can convey it with brevity. To watch him speak on TV makes it clear why he was captain at every level he played at and why Gatland made him Lions’ captain twice. Warburton’s arrival on the commentary scene came at a time when the rugby public was looking for more detail from their pundits. Gone were the days of just adding a bit of colour to commentary and chatting about the emotional aspects of the game. The public wanted technical analysis and Warburton rewrote the PowerPoint for the whole of rugby comms. Still the best in the business.

Rugby.title: Wales women name five uncapped players for Barbarians
Rugby.summary: Five players will make their senior debuts for Wales when they take on the Barbarians at Twickenham on Saturday (17:00 BST).
Rugby.body:
Five players will make their senior debuts for Wales when they take on the Barbarians at Allianz Stadium on Saturday (17:00 BST). Amy Williams and Kelsie Webster are named in the starting backline, while Stella Orrin, Lucy Isaac and Lily Hawkins are among the replacements.

Kayleigh Powell, who shone at full-back during the Six Nations, gets an opportunity at fly-half, with Lleucu George among a host of Premiership Women's Rugby (PWR) players either rested or unavailable for the uncapped fixture.

Rugby.title: Automotive brand ends PWR's two year wait for title sponsor with multi-million pound deal
Rugby.summary: Premiership Women's Rugby have ended their two year search for a title sponsor by securing a multi-million pound and multi-year deal with automotive brand, JAECOO.
Rugby.body:
Premiership Women’s Rugby have ended their two year search for a title sponsor by securing a multi-million pound, multi-year deal with automotive brand, JAECOO. The Chinese manufacturer is one of the fastest growing automotive brands in the United Kingdom and have already established themselves in rugby as the official automotive partner of European Professional Club Rugby [EPCR] and the For the Love of Rugby podcast hosted by ex-England internationals Ben Youngs and Dan Cole.

PWR has been without a title sponsor since the world’s largest insurance company, Allianz, stopped sponsoring the competition in 2024. From the start of the 2026/27 season, the competition will be names JAECOO PWR and the preseason competition will be renamed the JAECOO PWR Next Gen Cup.

Rugby.title: Morgan to make Wales men's return against Barbarians
Rugby.summary: Flanker Jac Morgan is back in Wales' starting line-up for Saturday's uncapped fixture against the Barbarians at Allianz Stadium, Twickenham.
Rugby.body:
Jac Morgan will make his Wales return as a starter against the Barbarians at Allianz Stadium on Saturday (14:00 BST). The British and Irish Lions flanker has been named at open-side for the uncapped fixture in Twickenham.

Morgan returns to the international stage after missing eight Tests because of a dislocated left shoulder suffered when scoring a try against Argentina in boss Steve Tandy's first game last autumn.

Rugby.title: 'It is going to ramp up': Stewart expects big changes in full-time Wallaroos set-up
Rugby.summary: Wallaroos wing Maya Stewart expects there to be plenty of changes in the programme when it is established as a full-time set-up.
Rugby.body:
Wallaroos wing Maya Stewart expects there to be plenty of changes in the programme when it is established as a full-time set-up. Earlier this week Olympic gold medal winning coach Tim Walsh was appointed by Rugby Australia as their Director of Women’s High Performance. Earlier this season the 26-year-old, who is the all-time leading try-scorer for the Wallaroos, spent time with Walsh’s sevens side.

Among the 47-year-old’s responsibilities include launching the Wallaroos as a full-time programme which will be based in Sydney as the side look for improved results in the lead up to their home Women’s Rugby World Cup in 2029.

Rugby.title: Siya Kolisi on the 2018 Bok game against England that 'changed my life'
Rugby.summary: As Siya Kolisi prepares to lead the Springboks for the 72nd time in the Nations Championship opener against England in Johannesburg...
Rugby.body:
As Siya Kolisi prepares to lead the Springboks for the 72nd time in the Nations Championship opener against England in Johannesburg, he has revealed the vital lessons he learnt while inspiring his team to a remarkable comeback victory in his first game as captain against the same opponents in 2018.

The Springboks were 24-3 down at one point but stunned England with a fight back that earned a 42-39 victory. “That 2018 match was a huge learning opportunity for me, and it obviously changed my life. I remember it was very tough and emotional,” Kolisi told local media ahead of the Ellis Park clash on July 4.” I think the emotions got the better of me. I struggled a bit, but the team did well, and I had so many great leaders around me.”

Rugby.title: The Boks and All Blacks prove it's time we think differently about JRLO
Rugby.summary: Phone +54 2122373655. No, its not a number somewhere in Argentina (although you could be forgiven for thinking so with plus 54 the nation’s international phone prefix).
Rugby.body:
No, its not a number somewhere in Argentina (although you could be forgiven for thinking so with plus 54 the nation’s international phone prefix). It’s actually the winning margins from each of the six matches of this year’s Super Rugby finals, with the average gap between the participants throughout the series, an astounding 37.5 points.

It was the most one-sided play-offs series in the competition’s 30-year history, perhaps indicating the need of something new, to at least jazz up the pointy end of the season.

Rugby.title: Cardiff captain Belcher out of Wales summer games
Rugby.summary: Cardiff hooker Liam Belcher is out of Wales' summer matches because of a neck injury.
Rugby.body:
Cardiff hooker Liam Belcher has been ruled out of Wales' summer matches because of a neck injury. Belcher, 30, was one of four hookers in the original squad named by head coach Steve Tandy alongside Cardiff club-mate Evan Lloyd, Scarlets front rower Ryan Elias and captain Dewi Lake.

Wales say Belcher suffered the injury during Cardiff's United Rugby Championship (URC) quarter-final play-off defeat against Stormers at the end of May and he has failed to recover in time.

Rugby.title: Wales lose another player for Nations Championship on eve of BaaBaas
Rugby.summary: Wales hooker Liam Belcher has become the latest player to withdraw from their squad for the Nations Championship campaign due to a neck injury sustained in Cardiff's United Rugby Championship defeat to the Stormers.
Rugby.body:
Wales hooker Liam Belcher has become the latest player to withdraw from their squad for the Nations Championship campaign due to a neck injury sustained in Cardiff’s United Rugby Championship defeat to the Stormers.

The 30-year-old picked up the injury in the second half of the 44-21 loss in Cape Town and has failed to recover in time to feature for his country against Fiji, Argentina and South Africa in July.

Rugby.title: Monaco heads to Moldova with promotion hopes alive in Rugby Sevens Championship
Rugby.summary: Monaco heads to Moldova with promotion hopes alive in Rugby Sevens Championship Monaco Life
Rugby.body:
After making history with a silver medal performance during the opening stage of the Rugby Sevens Trophy Championship in Makarska earlier this month, Monaco’s national sevens team will return to action from 26th to 28th June for the competition’s second stage in Chișinǎu, Moldova.

After already exceeding expectations in Croatia, marking an impressive return to Europe’s second-tier competition and establishing themselves as contenders for promotion, Monaco enters the tournament with its promotion ambitions firmly in place.

Rugby.title: The Welsh hooker preparing to play against Wales
Rugby.summary: Dragons hooker Elliot Dee will start for Barbarians against Wales at the Allianz Stadium on Saturday.
Rugby.body:
While George North has been getting most of the attention this week, it is another Welsh international who starts for Barbarians against Wales on Saturday. Hooker Elliot Dee will be in the Baa-Baas line-up for the uncapped match at the Allianz Stadium in Twickenham, while North will bring down his glittering professional career when he comes off the replacements bench.

The two Welshmen are rooming together in London as they prepare to face the country they have represented for more than a decade. "It's going to be interesting, something I've never done before.

Rugby.title: Wales and Barbarians ready to add to rich history
Rugby.summary: Wales and the Barbarians will add another chapter to their rich history in a double-header in London on Saturday.
Rugby.body:
Wales will add another chapter to the historic fixture against the Barbarians when playing a double-header in Twickenham at the end of the month. Steve Tandy's men will warm up for their Nations Championship Tests when facing the invitational side at 14:00 BST on Saturday, 27 June before Sean Lynn's women take to the field at Allianz Stadium at 17:00 BST.

The teams played a double-header in Cardiff seven years ago and now meet at the home of English rugby in an event hosted by Barbarians. Before the start of another summer of international rugby, BBC Sport Wales looks back at the historic fixture.

Rugby.title: Telusa Veainu: ‘There’s something spiritual, a feeling of home when I go back to the Tonga team’
Rugby.summary: The veteran full-back thought his country "might have run out of players" when he was recalled for the Nations Cup, but is relishing his return.
Rugby.body:
The trio of Telusa Veainu, Logo Mulipola and Semesa Rokoduguni have an affectionate nickname among their younger peers at Doncaster. Having ‘the pensioners’ around, combined age 112, comes with considerable benefits, passing on what it takes to play at the top.

“We’re all mentally the same age [as the younger players], but physically, we are all probably feeling it a lot more, taking a lot more time to recover,” Veainu admits. There will be little opportunity to rest for the 35-year-old this summer. A recall by Tonga to play in the Nations Cup, two years after Veainu’s last cap against Spain, came as a most welcome surprise.

Rugby.title: Beating the Barbarians would be everything
Rugby.summary: Wales head coach Sean Lynn says beating the Barbarians on Saturday would mean everything to his side.
Rugby.body:
Wales head coach Sean Lynn says beating the Barbarians on Saturday would mean everything to his side. Wales head to Allianz Stadium on a nine Test losing streak and will be without some of their Premiership Women's Rugby (PWR) stars for the double-header involving the Wales and Baa-baas men's teams.

It means Lynn will look to Wales' next generation of talent as they prepare to come up against some megastars of the women's game. "You look at 10, 12, 13, they're very exciting for the Barbarians and then you've got Ruby Tui who's going to be explosive," said Lynn.

Rugby.title: 'Devastating' Bermudez signs on for more with Loughborough
Rugby.summary: Canada wing Fancy Bermudez claimed she had found a "sense of family" upon re-signing for Premiership Women's Rugby club Loughborough Lightning.
Rugby.body:
Canada wing Fancy Bermudez claimed she had found a “sense of family” upon re-signing for Premiership Women’s Rugby club Loughborough Lightning. The 24-year-old made 12 appearances in her first season with the East Midlanders and scored 12 tries. Loughborough finished sixth in the PWR table and enjoyed a bright start to the campaign.

Bermudez also spent chunks of the season with Canada Women’s Sevens and competed in HSBC SVNS Series and World Championship competitions. “My main reason for re-signing was the culture and this environment,” Bermudez said. “It has been so refreshing for me; I’ve only been on a select few teams that have given me a sense of family.

Rugby.title: 'She is a natural leader': USA international to exit Trailfinders
Rugby.summary: Experienced USA Women's Eagles back-row Kate Zackary will leave Trailfinders Women after the Premiership Women's Rugby Final this Sunday.
Rugby.body:
Experienced USA Women’s Eagles back-row Kate Zackary will leave Trailfinders Women after the Premiership Women’s Rugby Final this Sunday. The 37-year-old forward, who has represented her country at three Women’s Rugby World Cups, has spent the last three seasons in West London and has been a key player for Barney Maddison’s team.

Zackary joined Trailfinders after three years with Exeter Chiefs in Devon. Upon her arrival in London the forward became a fan favourite and could cap her time with the team with an English league title at the Twickenham Stoop this weekend.

Rugby.title: Eagles set New York date with Red Roses for WXV Global Series finale
Rugby.summary: England's Red Roses will conclude their 2026 WXV Global Series campaign against the USA Women's Eagles at Sports Illustrated Stadium in October.
Rugby.body:
England’s Red Roses will conclude their 2026 WXV Global Series campaign against the USA Women’s Eagles at Sports Illustrated Stadium in October. The fixture will be the sixth Test match that the Women’s Rugby World Cup 2025 champions play across the eight-week WXV Global Series window. In the fortnight before they are hosted by the Eagles, England will play two matches against Canada.

Last August, England and the USA played in the Women’s Rugby World Cup’s opening fixture at Sunderland’s Stadium of Light. The fixture set a tournament record attendance with more than 42,000 in the stands for the Red Roses’ 69-7 win.

Rugby.title: Portugal squad missing two title-winning heroes but legend returns
Rugby.summary: Portugal are heading to North America with a star-packed roster, as Samuel Marques, Manuel Vareiro, Cody Thomas, Vincent Pinto, and José Madeira are all set to feature for the Lobos at the inaugural World Rugby Nations Cup.
Rugby.body:
Portugal are heading to North America with a star-packed roster, as Samuel Marques, Manuel Vareiro, Cody Thomas, Vincent Pinto, and José Madeira are all set to feature for the Lobos at the inaugural World Rugby Nations Cup.

With Portugal’s training camp having started two weeks ago, head coach Simon Mannix has only been able to count on his key players since Monday, with US Carcassonne’s Marques already working with the group.

Rugby.title: Munster sign Australian playmaker to 'add balance to the squad'
Rugby.summary: Munster Rugby have confirmed the signing of Australian Will Harrison from Japanese side Kinetsu Liners on a one-year deal.
Rugby.body:
Munster Rugby have confirmed the signing of Australian utility back Will Harrison from Japanese side Kinetsu Liners on a one-year deal. The left-footed playmaker, who is capable of playing at fly-half, full-back and centre, is a former Australian Schooboys and U20s captain, who led the Junior Wallabies to the final of the World Rugby Junior World Championship in 2019.

The youngest-ever player to debut for the NSW Waratahs in 2020, Harrison scored 257 points in 38 appearances for the Super Rugby side before making the move to Japan in 2024. “I’m extremely excited to be joining such a proud province. From my first conversations with the club, it felt like a place I wanted to be,” said Harrison.

Rugby.title: ‘The hope is that it’s not a cruel summer for Wales and the good work of the Six Nations is not undone’
Rugby.summary: History underlines how daunting a task Wales face in the coming weeks, when Steve Tandy will need his leading lights to point the way.
Rugby.body:
A lurch in form for Wales in the late 1980s prompted a Gren cartoon in the old Saturday evening Football Echo which saw the Ponty an’ Pop characters walk past a billboard that read: ‘Welsh trial match: Those Out Of Form v Those Out Of Position’.

Those were the days, my friend, an era when Wales lost to Bridgend and changed coaches almost as often as the modern-day UK changes prime ministers. Let’s all agree tough times are not new to the game on the western side of the River Severn, then.

Rugby.title: Two World Cup-winning Springboks to start for Barbarians against Wales
Rugby.summary: Faf de Klerk will captain the Barbarians when they take on Wales at Twickenham's Allianz Stadium on Saturday, a week after helping South Africa beat the invitational side in Gqeberha.
Rugby.body:
Faf de Klerk will captain the Barbarians when they take on Wales at Twickenham’s Allianz Stadium on Saturday, a week after helping South Africa beat the invitational side in Gqeberha.

The two-time Rugby World Cup winner scored off the bench in the Springboks’ 80-31 victory last weekend, but will now lead his former opponents in a side featuring 19 internationals from 11 different nations.

The two-time Rugby World Cup winner scored off the bench in the Springboks’ 80-31 victory last weekend, but will now lead his former opponents in a side featuring 19 internationals from 11 different nations.

Rugby.title: Springbok call-ups inspire South Africa U20s skipper to follow their lead
Rugby.summary: South Africa start their Junior World Championship title defence against Uruguay in Tbilisi this Saturday.
Rugby.body:
When multiple World Cup winner Handrè Pollard broke into the Springbok team and made his debut less than a fortnight after helping South Africa’s U20 team reach the final of the World Rugby Junior World Championship in 2014, it was further evidence that age is no barrier to Test selection.

The pathway between the U20s international rugby and the senior level is well-worn, with Pollard joined on that journey by some notable U20 teammates from that class of 2014, such as Thomas du Toit, Jesse Kriel and Andre Esterhuizen, amongst others, and many more since.

Rugby.title: 'I needed a new environment... you get to learn a lot off them'
Rugby.summary: Dillon Lewis says he never gave up on playing for Wales as he prepares to end a spell of over two years in the international wilderness.
Rugby.body:
Dillon Lewis says he never gave up on playing for Wales as he prepares to end a spell of over two years in the international wilderness. The 57-cap tighthead prop helped Wales win the Six Nations title in 2019 and played at two World Cups.

But the 30-year-old – whose last appearance came against Italy in March 2024 – has been recalled to Steve Tandy’s squad for Wales’ summer programme. “Two years and it’s been riddled with a few injuries as well which hasn’t helped my case, but it was never something I gave up on,” said Lewis, the former Cardiff strongman who signed for Dragons last summer after a two-year spell in England with Harlequins.

Rugby.title: 2026 PWR Final: What do previous Saracens and Trailfinders encounters tell us?
Rugby.summary: Saracens versus Trailfinders Women. Come Sunday afternoon at 5pm, one of those two clubs will be celebrating at the Twickenham Stoop.
Rugby.body:
Saracens versus Trailfinders Women. Come Sunday afternoon at 5pm, one of those two clubs will be celebrating at the Twickenham Stoop, having overcome every single challenge to sit atop Premiership Women’s Rugby’s iron throne.

Trailfinders, who made the ‘impossible’ feat of toppling Gloucester Hartpury from clinching a four-peat, have managed to beat the odds and qualify for the knockouts for the first time since they joined the PWR in 2023.

Rugby.title: Lewis revives Wales career in problem position
Rugby.summary: Dillon Lewis is back in the international fold after a two-year absence as Wales look to solve their problems at tight-head prop.
Rugby.body:
Two years on from his most recent international outing, tight-head prop Dillon Lewis is looking to help fill Wales' problem position. The 30-year-old earned an international recall for the summer schedule against Fiji, Argentina and South Africa in the Nations Championship.

Wales tune up for those Tests with an uncapped fixture against Barbarians at Allianz Stadium on Saturday (14:00 BST). It presents Lewis with the opportunity to represent his country for the first time since winning his 57th cap against Italy in the 2024 Six Nations.

Rugby.title: England 'incredibly happy' to rest Itoje against South Africa
Rugby.summary: England assistant Richard Wigglesworth says leaving Maro Itoje out of next month's game with world champions South Africa is the right decision.
Rugby.body:
Leaving captain Maro Itoje out of next month's titanic meeting with world champions South Africa is the correct decision, says England senior assistant coach Richard Wigglesworth.

Itoje has been rested for the 4 July match against the Springboks as well as Tests against Fiji and Argentina on subsequent weekends in order to give him extended time away from rugby.

Rugby.title: Canada call up six uncapped players for Nations Cup
Rugby.summary: Canada head coach Stephen Meehan has named his 32-man squad for next month's Nations Cup, with a blend of experienced internationals and six uncapped players.
Rugby.body:
Canada head coach Stephen Meehan has named his 32-man squad for next month’s Nations Cup, with a blend of experienced internationals and six uncapped players. The Canucks open their campaign against Spain in Edmonton on July 4 before facing Portugal and Zimbabwe later in the month.

Several senior figures return to the fold, including former captain Tyler Ardron, lock Evan Olmstead and hooker Andrew Quattrin, while Lucas Rumball rejoins the national side fresh from captaining the Chicago Hounds to the Major League Rugby title.

Several senior figures return to the fold, including former captain Tyler Ardron, lock Evan Olmstead and hooker Andrew Quattrin, while Lucas Rumball rejoins the national side fresh from captaining the Chicago Hounds to the Major League Rugby title.

Rugby.title: 'It can’t be an excuse for us': England embrace 'massive challenge'
Rugby.summary: England depart for their summer tour on Wednesday with a no-excuses policy, despite embarking on a 25,000-mile itinerary spanning three continents.
Rugby.body:
England depart for their summer tour on Wednesday with a no-excuses policy, despite embarking on a 25,000-mile itinerary spanning three continents. Steve Borthwick’s team face Tests against South Africa in Johannesburg, Fiji in Liverpool and Argentina in Santiago del Estero in the opening phase of the inaugural Nations Championship, the new biennial competition based around the July and November international windows.

England expect to have a clean bill of health for their opener against back-to-back world champions the Springboks on July 4 and are approaching the coming weeks as an opportunity to prove their resilience.

Rugby.title: 'That's a better competition for us to play in': WRU aiming for two teams in PWR
Rugby.summary: Welsh Rugby Union director of women's rugby Nadine Griffiths has revealed that two Welsh teams could join Premiership Women's Rugby.
Rugby.body:
Welsh Rugby Union director of women’s rugby Nadine Griffiths has revealed that two Welsh teams could join Premiership Women’s Rugby. Griffiths, who officially started her her role with the WRU in June, said in a BBC Sport interview that two teams from Wales, Scotland and Ireland could join PWR to create a 15-team competition from the 2027/28 season.

Currently the three nations compete in the six-team Celtic Challenge competition, which has been exclusively won by Irish teams since the league’s inception in 2023. In March, the English competition announced they would accept expressions of interest. Over the remainder of the Guinness Women’s Six Nations, it was confirmed by Irish, Scottish and Welsh unions that they had registered their interest with the competition.

In March, the English competition announced they would accept expressions of interest. Over the remainder of the Guinness Women’s Six Nations, it was confirmed by Irish, Scottish and Welsh unions that they had registered their interest with the competition.

Rugby.title: 10 Prem stars named in Argentina's squad; 91-cap veteran misses out
Rugby.summary: Argentina head coach Felipe Contepomi has named five uncapped players in his 34-strong squad for the upcoming Nations Championship fixtures against Scotland, Wales and England.
Rugby.body:
Argentina head coach Felipe Contepomi has named five uncapped players in his 34-strong squad for the upcoming Nations Championship fixtures against Scotland, Wales and England.

One of the quintet is Western Force hooker Leonel Oviedo, the older brother of established number eight, Joaquín Oviedo. The others are domestic-based players in Super Rugby Americas – Luciano Asevedo and Juan Penoucos in the forwards and Agustín Fraga and Mateo Soler in the backs.

Rugby.title: Kennewell calls time on career after 'picture-perfect' World Championship victory
Rugby.summary: Demi Kennewell has officially confirmed her retirement after a highly successful 10-year career in the Aussie 7s, bowing out as a world champion.
Rugby.body:
Demi Kennewell (née Hayes) has officially confirmed her retirement after a highly successful 10-year career in the Aussie Women’s Sevens, bowing out as a world champion. Among Kennewell’s many achievements include 13 HSBC SVNS Series titles, a Commonwealth Games gold medal, a Rugby Sevens World Cup gold medal and featuring as part of the 2026 side that won the HSBC SVNS World Championship earlier this year.

Originally from Mount Isa, Kennewell joined the Australian Youth Sevens in 2015, debuting in the programme in 2016 at the Canada Sevens, where she played her first match against Brazil.

Rugby.title: Reds snag Tigers and Blues assistants to finalise Cotter's coaching ticket
Rugby.summary: The Queensland Reds have confirmed Vern Cotter's coaching ticket, with Peter Hewat and Craig McGrath joining the Kiwi at Ballymore.
Rugby.body:
The Queensland Reds have confirmed Vern Cotter’s coaching ticket, with Peter Hewat and Craig McGrath joining the Kiwi at Ballymore. The pair have signed two-year deals to join Cotter and Zane Hilton in the new Reds setup, with McGrath following the head coach across the Tasman from the Blues while Hewat joins from a two-year stint with Leicester Tigers.

A former Waratahs and London Irish flyer, Hewat returns to the state he called home during his schooling at Nudgee College, having tasted Super Rugby coaching with the Brumbies from 2018-2020, and previously coached a handful of Reds stars at the U18 representative level.

Rugby.title: Laetitia Royer column: 'I am ready to be fully myself in the PWR Final'
Rugby.summary: You can feel emotion creeping into people’s voice when they talk about the Premiership Women’s Rugby Final now.
Rugby.body:
You can feel emotion creeping into people’s voice when they talk about the Premiership Women’s Rugby Final now. They want to win it. They want to go for the W. We’re going to step up. It is just right for Saracens to win it.

There is a lot of emotion. Every leader wants to talk more. Every leader wants to say more things. Personally, I’ve had some small injuries this season and I have been mainly focused on managing all that. That has really been the story of my season. Before the Christmas break I had only made three appearances in PWR. Now I feel more asserted in the team. I won’t be finishing this season on a personal low.

Rugby.title: Wallabies' winless tour still burns as chance to 'rectify' arrives
Rugby.summary: Still smarting seven months on, the Wallabies are intent on wiping the slate clean and starting the year with a bang in a sellout blockbuster against Ireland in Sydney.
Rugby.body:
Still smarting seven months on, the Wallabies are intent on wiping the slate clean and starting the year with a bang in a sellout blockbuster against Ireland in Sydney. Former captain Allan Alaalatoa is among the Wallabies stalwarts who endured the first four-Test winless tour of Europe since 1959 with successive losses to England, Italy, Ireland and France last November.

Alaalatoa remembers the gloomy tour like it was yesterday and admits the motivation to atone on July 4 and set the Wallabies back on the path to World Cup contention on home soil next year is driving the squad.

Rugby.title: Selections aside, Dave Rennie is pushing the right buttons with All Blacks
Rugby.summary: Everyone wants winners and losers out of any team naming. Frankly, when the net’s cast this wide, those kinds of categories are redundant.
Rugby.body:
The All Blacks’ 1987 Rugby World Cup-winning squad consisted of 26 players. It’s hard not to think of that when you see an initial squad of 34 picked to play the first three Tests of this season and contemplate that 44 players will then tour South Africa.

Everyone wants winners and losers out of any team naming. Frankly, when the net’s cast this wide, those kinds of categories are redundant. Forty-five men played Test rugby for New Zealand in 2025. So, if you missed the 34 to play France, Italy and Ireland, there’s every chance you’ll be in the 44 that goes to South Africa.

Rugby.title: Fit-again Callender to lead Wales against Barbarians
Rugby.summary: Fit-again Alex Callender to lead from the front when Wales take on the Barbarians at Allianz Stadium on Saturday.
Rugby.body:
Alex Callender fully intends to make the most of a "bonus" fixture when leading Wales out against the Barbarians on Saturday. The influential back-row forward will captain Sean Lynn's side when they play the second fixture of the double-header at Allianz Stadium in Twickenham, taking to the field at 17:00 BST after the men's game at 14:00 BST.

Callender will feature for Wales for the first time since last year's World Cup after an ankle injury forced her to miss the Six Nations. "I put no pressure on myself to come back for a game," said Callender. "This would've been a bonus if fit, so I am looking forward to the weekend.

Rugby.title: Ardie Savea details what success will look like for him as All Blacks captain
Rugby.summary: An admittedly nervous yet excited Ardie Savea was unveiled as All Blacks captain this week, sharing what the recognition means to him and what he hopes to achieve in the role.
Rugby.body:
An admittedly nervous yet excited Ardie Savea was unveiled as All Blacks captain this week, sharing what the recognition means to him and what he hopes to achieve in the role.

The All Blacks centurion has served as vice-captain for the past few seasons and stepped in as captain on numerous occasions, but taking on the responsibility full-time is new. The fan-favourite to assume the role, Savea succeeds Scott Barrett as captain, with the Crusaders lock having led for two years under Scott Robertson.

Rugby.title: Moana Pasifika officially out of 2027 Super Rugby Pacific season
Rugby.summary: New Zealand Rugby has confirmed that Moana Pasifika will not compete in the 2027 Super Rugby Pacific season.
Rugby.body:
New Zealand Rugby has confirmed that Moana Pasifika will not compete in the 2027 Super Rugby Pacific season. The announcement comes after the governing body, which controls Moana Pasifika’s licence to participate in the competition, concluded its exploration of “every possibility for a long-term solution” but was left unconvinced that the options would satisfy its criteria.

Multiple parties were involved in the process, but even after NZR pushed back its initial May 15 deadline, the capital and business plan requirements remained unmet. NZR outlined the requirements as 1) A credible business plan demonstrating the capacity to generate commercial revenue to operate on a sustainable basis over the long term, and 2) Evidence of sufficient committed capital to meet establishment costs, support operations in the short-term, and provide resilience against financial risks.

Rugby.title: On form it's Ruben Love then daylight for the All Blacks
Rugby.summary: The All Blacks coaching staff have reiterated that selections were made on form but when it comes to the No.10s it's Ruben Love then daylight.
Rugby.body:
When it comes to the All Blacks crop of No.10s selected Monday it’s Ruben Love then daylight. Veterans Damian McKenzie and Beauden Barrett have also been selected, as expected, but neither can match Love on form.

McKenzie had a nightmare performance in the Super Rugby final, but even before that, the 31-year-old wasn’t producing at the type of plays that made him a sensation 10 years ago. He’s in a different stage of his career where he should be providing the steady hand, not the one orchestrating an implosion.

Rugby.title: Remaining 2026 Black Ferns schedule confirmed, with free exhibition game added
Rugby.summary: All fixtures for the Black Ferns' expanded international season have now been finalised, with a free-to-attend exhibition match added to the slate of home games in August.
Rugby.body:
All fixtures for the Black Ferns’ expanded international season have now been finalised, with a free-to-attend exhibition match added to the slate of home games in August. Having already taken out the 2026 Pacific Four Series with three bonus-point wins in April, the team will next take the field on August 14 against the Black Ferns XV.

The exhibition match will be held in Upper Hutt, pitting the country’s top talent against those snapping at their heels, and will be free for fans to attend. While the rest of the international fixtures will be streamed live on Sky Sport, the Black Ferns XV match will also be free to watch on the Black Ferns’ YouTube and blackferns.com.

Rugby.title: North prepares for one last dance against Wales
Rugby.summary: George North will finish his sparkling rugby career on Saturday when he plays for the Barbarians against his country Wales at the Allianz Stadium in Twickenham.
Rugby.body:
George North will have one more opportunity to grace the rugby field this weekend when he lines up for the Barbarians against Wales. For a man who scored 47 tries in 121 internationals for his country, it will take some reconciliation that his final professional game will be in the black and white Baa-Baas jersey against the nation he served with such distinction for 14 years.

If it will be challenging for the man himself, who announced in April he will retire at the end of this season, North says it has been a particular struggle trying to explain the situation to his two sons, Jac and Tomi.

Rugby.title: Cardiff sign Tonga's Paea from Moana Pasifika
Rugby.summary: Cardiff sign Tonga back-five forward Semisi Paea from Super Rugby Pacific side Moana Pasifika
Rugby.body:
Cardiff have signed Tonga back-five forward Semisi Paea from Super Rugby Pacific side Moana Pasifika as they prepare for the 2026-27 season. The 27-year-old can play blindside flanker or lock and the Blue and Blacks also note his versatility across the back row.

Paea said: "This is an exciting new chapter in my rugby journey and I am thrilled to be joining such a well-respected club as Cardiff." With similarly gifted Wales forward Christ Tshiunza having opted to move from Exeter to Sale Sharks instead of seeking a return to his home city, Paea's arrival plugs a gap for Cardiff.

Rugby.title: The verdict on every women's team after 2026 SVNS World Championship
Rugby.summary: RugbyPass takes a final look at the twelve teams that took part in the 2026 HSBC SVNS World Championship.
Rugby.body:
The 2025-26 HSBC SVNS World Championship has come to its end, with the twelve teams across the men’s and women’s tournaments breaking for the next few months before preparations begin for 2026-27.

The women’s tournament saw a thrilling contest, with the finals ultimately coming down to a two-way horse race between Australia and New Zealand. It took until the final match of the season to split the teams – but the rivalry overshadowed a year where there were plenty of other great stories coming out of the women’s series.

Rugby.title: Borthwick’s ‘Burgess call’; Pollock to start? Chessum at six? Caluori unleashed? Five key issues from England squad
Rugby.summary: Can Janse van Rensburg win over doubters? Is it Pollock's time to start? Will England play three locks v Boks? Is Caluori ready?
Rugby.body:
England head coach Steve Borthwick has settled on his men for the inaugural Nations Championship, which they kick off against world champions South Africa in Johannesburg on 4 July.

RugbyPass assesses five key issues to emerge from the 36-man squad named on Monday. Resting Maro Itoje is a smart long-term move. He hasn’t been the same force this season on the back of a cumulative rugby workload which would have left Hercules sagging. He has also had to deal with the loss of his mum. Some proper time away from the game will be beneficial for him and England down the line.

Rugby.title: 'If I were coaching him...': Rassie Erasmus likens Henry Pollock to Bok superstar
Rugby.summary: South Africa head coach Rassie Erasmus has said that the only thing that counts regarding England back-row Henry Pollock is what he does on the field, describing the 21-year-old's output as "exceptional".
Rugby.body:
South Africa head coach Rassie Erasmus has said that the only thing that counts regarding England back-row Henry Pollock is what he does on the field, describing the 21-year-old’s output as “exceptional”.

Fresh from a player of the match performance as Northampton Saints won the Gallagher PREM final against Exeter Chiefs on Saturday, Pollock is expected to play a starring role in England’s Nations Championship campaign, which begins for Steve Borthwick’s side against the Springboks at Ellis Park on July 5.

Rugby.title: Proud Warrior Dobie commits to long-term deal at Scotstoun
Rugby.summary: Scotland scrum-half/wing Jamie Dobie has signed a long-term contract extension with Glasgow Warriors.
Rugby.body:
Scotland scrum-half/wing Jamie Dobie has signed a long-term contract extension with Glasgow Warriors. The new deal, understood to be for three years until the summer of 2029, comes as the 25-year-old approaches a century of games in Glasgow colours, having made 99 appearances in his seven years with the club to date.

Dobie made his senior professional debut in the opening match of the 2019/20 season, coming off the bench away to the Toyota Cheetahs after joining the Warriors directly from Merchiston Castle School.

Rugby.title: England wing Venner to stay at Gloucester-Hartpury
Rugby.summary: England winger Mia Venner signs a new deal to stay with Gloucester-Hartpury.
Rugby.body:
England winger Mia Venner has signed a new deal to stay with Gloucester-Hartpury. Venner joined the Premiership Women's Rugby (PWR) side in 2019 and last season played in every one of their games, while her tally of 12 tries is the joint highest in the league.

The 24-year-old has been capped four times by the Red Roses and scored two tries as part of this year's Six Nations Grand Slam-winning squad. "[Mia] brings some outstanding qualities both on and off the field, which make her a hugely valuable member of the squad," said head of rugby Dan Murphy.

Rugby.title: Gloucester Hartpury 'delighted' to retain 'key figure' for 2026/27
Rugby.summary: England international Mia Venner has signed a new deal with her club side Gloucester Hartpury.
Rugby.body:
England Red Roses wing Mia Venner has signed a new deal with her club side Gloucester Hartpury. The 24-year-old first joined the West Country side in 2019 while she was still in school and studied at Hartpury University. As a 17-year the wing, who can also play full-back, scored a hat-trick on her debut for the club.

Later that season she was called into the senior Red Roses’ camp and scored a try on her Test debut against Wales in the 2020 Guinness Women’s Six Nations. This term Venner featured in every Gloucester Hartpury fixture across Premiership Women’s Rugby and the PWR Cup. In the league she scored 10 tries, beat 76 defenders and made 1,066 metres.

Rugby.title: Lake to captain Wales in summer internationals
Rugby.summary: The Gloucester-bound hooker will captain Wales in this summer's four internationals, stating with Saturday's game against the Barbarians at Allianz Stadium.
Rugby.body:
Wales have named hooker Dewi Lake as captain for their summer internationals ahead of fit-again flanker Jac Morgan. Lake led the side for eight Tests in the 2025 autumn series and 2026 Six Nations while Morgan was injured.

The British and Irish Lions flanker captained Wales in the first game of the Steve Tandy era against Argentina but dislocated his left shoulder when scoring a try. The Gloucester-bound Ospreys have both been named in the squad for the summer internationals, which start against the Barbarians at Allianz Stadium in Twickenham on Saturday, and Lake will be skipper.

Rugby.title: George North: 'My friends have had rugby taken away
Rugby.summary: The Welsh icon reflects on a glittering career, hoisting Israel Folau and his turmoil with concussion as he prepares to play his final match.
Rugby.body:
Few athletes, no matter how celebrated, are afforded the ultimate send-off, carried from the field on the shoulders of their comrades, medal round the neck, trophy held aloft, sailing into the sunset on an ocean of champagne.

Sitting on the sidelines in the south of France, any sort of farewell at all seemed remote for George North. This great totem of the game was destined to see out his career from the stands as his Provence side came within a match of promotion to the Top 14. For various reasons, North has not played since February. The Welsh icon watched, unfulfilled and stymied, from the periphery and made his peace with the fact this was how it would end.

Rugby.title: Sale Sharks recruit 'most underrated' player in PWR
Rugby.summary: Sale Sharks boss Tom Hudson described new signing Freya Aucken as one of Premiership Women's Rugby's "most underrated" players when the North West club announced her signing.
Rugby.body:
Sale Sharks boss Tom Hudson described new signing Freya Aucken as one of Premiership Women’s Rugby‘s “most underrated” players when the North West club announced her signing. The 24-year-old has joined Sale from Harlequins, who the back three player and scrum-half has represented since the 2020/21 Premier 15s season. She has represented the West London club on 81 occasions and scored five tries in the 2025/26 season.

Hudson said: “Freya is arguably one of the most underrated players in the PWR. “With her ability to be equally at home on the wing or at scrum half and with one PWR title already under her belt we are massively excited to be bringing her North and for her to join our Sharks family. I can’t wait to see what we can achieve together.”

Rugby.title: Top 14 star among several high-profile omissions from Uruguay squad
Rugby.summary: Uruguay have revealed their 33-player roster for the upcoming World Rugby Nations Cup, with the Teros missing several of their main starters.
Rugby.body:
Uruguay have revealed their 33-player roster for the upcoming World Rugby Nations Cup, with the Teros missing several of their main starters. Castres scrum-half Santiago Arata, who was called up by the Barbarians this month, is one of the biggest omissions from the playing squad.

Arata, 29, is one of the few Uruguayans in history to play as a full-time professional in a major European league, and has been capped 42 times. Regular skipper Manuel Leindekar is also among the absentees, with the captain nursing a minor injury and focusing on recovering in time to join his new club, FC Grenoble, for pre-season.

Rugby.title: Antoine Dupont missing for now as Gathie names 33-man France squad
Rugby.summary: Fabien Galthie has named his 33-man France squad for their Nations Championship fixtures against the All Blacks, Australia and Japan next month, with try-scoring maestro Louis Bielle-Biarrey not included.
Rugby.body:
Fabien Galthie has named his 33-man France squad for their Nations Championship fixtures against the All Blacks, Australia and Japan next month, with try-scoring maestro Louis Bielle-Biarrey not included.

The 23-year-old, who followed up being crowned the Six Nations player of the Championship with the Investec player of the year accolade, will be given a rest this summer, which had been widely reported, but a number of his Bordeaux-Begles team-mates have been included following their failure to make the Top 14 play-offs.

Rugby.title: Ex-Wallaby Top 14 winger set to return to rugby league
Rugby.summary: Former Wallaby winger Suliasi Vunivalu, who has been released by La Rochelle, is poised to switch back to rugby league and has been lined up for a move to Super League outfit Hull.
Rugby.body:
Former Wallaby winger Suliasi Vunivalu, who has been released by La Rochelle, is poised to switch back to rugby league and has been lined up for a move to Super League outfit Hull.

Vunivalu, 30, scored two tries in four games before the Champions Cup defeat 42-21 to the Stormers in December, when he ruptured his ACL and Ronan O’Gara called time on his stay on France’s Atlantic coast.

Rugby.title: Winners and losers from Dave Rennie’s first All Blacks squad
Rugby.summary: Hurricanes trio Xavier Numia, Josh Moorby, and Fehi Fineanganofo have receieved their maiden call-up, alongside German-born loose-forward Anton Segner.
Rugby.body:
Rennie’s 34-man squad was named on Monday at Fielding Rugby Club in Manawatu, ahead of three Nations Championship fixtures in New Zealand, starting on July 4. The new selection panel, including Rennie, Sir Graham Henry, and senior assistant Neil Barnes, picked four debutants in their first squad of 2026.

Hurricanes trio Xavier Numia, Josh Moorby, and Fehi Fineanganofo received their maiden call-up, alongside German-born loose-forward Anton Segner. The four inclusions have squeezed out a number of experienced All Blacks, and players who many expected to be named.

Hurricanes trio Xavier Numia, Josh Moorby, and Fehi Fineanganofo received their maiden call-up, alongside German-born loose-forward Anton Segner. The four inclusions have squeezed out a number of experienced All Blacks, and players who many expected to be named.

Rugby.title: 'We are building something genuinely special': Red Roses agree new contract framework with RFU
Rugby.summary: The RFU and England's Red Roses have agreed a new four-year contract framework that covers the current Women's Rugby World Cup cycle.
Rugby.body:
The RFU and England’s Red Roses have agreed a new four-year contract framework that covers the current Women’s Rugby World Cup cycle. English rugby’s governing body and the world champion side worked collaboratively with Team England Rugby to agree the new framework, which exists between 2026 and 2030.

In their press release, the RFU said: “This agreement represents a significant further investment in England’s women’s rugby programme, reinforcing the RFU’s long-term commitment to building a world-leading performance environment while accelerating the global growth of the women’s game.”

Rugby.title: Attack coach Gleeson leaves Bath
Rugby.summary: Bath attack coach Martin Gleeson leaves the club after eight months in the role.
Rugby.body:
Bath backs and attack coach Martin Gleeson has left the club after eight months in the role. Gleeson, a former England and Great Britain rugby league centre and two-time Super League winner, joined Bath in October a week after he left a coaching role at Warrington Wolves.

"I'd like to thank Johann for the opportunity this season to come to Bath and work with some amazing staff and talented players," Gleeson said. "It's been a great season and I wish the club all the success in the future."

Rugby.title: Quins poach Super Rugby champions' attack coach
Rugby.summary: Harlequins appoint Brad Cooper as their new attack coach, as they reshape their backroom staff for the 2026-27 season.
Rugby.body:
Harlequins have appointed Brad Cooper as their new attack coach, as they continue to reshape their backroom staff for the 2026-27 season. Cooper joins from Super Rugby champions Hurricanes, who averaged 41 points per game during the regular season, and scored 114 tries on their way to the trophy.

"It's clear there's a real ambition here to play rugby with tempo, and that's exactly the environment I want to be part of," Cooper told the club's website, external. "This is a great chance to help shape an attacking identity at a club with such history, and test myself as a coach in the northern hemisphere in the Gallagher Prem."

Rugby.title: Red Roses stars in line for £100K to retain World Cup
Rugby.summary: The Red Roses' top stars stand to earn £100,000 with England in 2029 should they successfully defend their Women's Rugby World Cup title in Australia.
Rugby.body:
The Red Roses' top stars stand to earn £100,000 with England in 2029 should they successfully defend their Women's Rugby World Cup title in Australia as part of a new four-year pay agreement with the Rugby Football Union.

Overall, it is understood the players have negotiated a 25% increase on their previous terms that will expire at the end of June. In addition to lifting the World Cup in front of a sellout 81,885 crowd in September, England have won eight successive Six Nations titles and are on a run of 38 successive Test victories.

Rugby.title: Bath Rugby have a coaching spot to fill after departure confirmed
Rugby.summary: Bath Rugby have confirmed that backs and attack coach Martin Gleeson has left the club after only one season in the role.
Rugby.body:
Bath Rugby have confirmed that backs and attack coach Martin Gleeson has left the club after only one season in the role. Gleeson joined Bath in October 2025, once Lee Blackett started work as England’s new attack coach.

While 102 tries were scored in the Prem season, matching the previous season’s return, Bath’s attack didn’t seem to fire as well under the rugby league legend, who is now looking for his next coaching gig.

Rugby.title: Faf de Klerk set for immediate international return after Springboks axe
Rugby.summary: South Africa scrum-half Faf de Klerk has been named in the Barbarians matchday squad to take on Wales at Twickenham's Allianz Stadium on Saturday, just days after missing out on Rassie Erasmus' 46-man Springboks squad for the Nations Championship.
Rugby.body:
South Africa scrum-half Faf de Klerk has been named in the Barbarians matchday squad to take on Wales at Twickenham’s Allianz Stadium on Saturday, just days after missing out on Rassie Erasmus’ 46-man Springboks squad for the Nations Championship.

The 34-year-old featured from the bench in the Boks’ 80-31 win over the BaaBaas at the Nelson Mandela Bay Stadium last Saturday, but failed to make the cut for their squad, which was named soon after.

Rugby.title: Bristol sign Wales back row Lewis
Rugby.summary: Bristol Bears sign Wales back row forward Bethan Lewis for the 2026-27 season following her departure from Gloucester-Hartpury.
Rugby.body:
Bristol Bears have signed Wales back row forward Bethan Lewis for the 2026-27 season following her departure from Gloucester-Hartpury. Lewis has been capped 65 times by Wales and captained the squad during this year's Women's Six Nations.

The 27-year-old won three Premiership Women's Rugby (PWR) titles with Gloucester-Hartpury, having joined the club in 2018 where she made 95 appearances. "The club has a clear vision for where it wants to go and after speaking with Scott [Lawson] and the coaching team, it is a journey that I'm keen to be a part of," Lewis said.

Rugby.title: ‘Two formats, one system’: Walsh hungry to bring winning sevens culture to Wallaroos
Rugby.summary: Few individuals have spent more time as involved with Australian women’s rugby than Tim Walsh, the head coach first getting his start with the sevens side in 2013, being at the head of some of Australian rugby’s biggest successes over the last decade.
Rugby.body:
Few individuals have spent more time as involved with Australian women’s rugby than Tim Walsh. The 47-year-old got his start with the sevens team in 2013 and has been at the heart of Australian rugby’s biggest success over the past decade.

Now, as Rugby Australia’s new Director of Women’s High Performance, Walsh has an opportunity to transform the footprint of Australian women’s rugby for generations to come. Australian women’s rugby is set for a colossal six years. The Aussie Women’s Sevens, fresh off their 2026 HSBC SVNS World Championship win, are firmly focused on the 2028 Summer Olympics in Los Angeles and a home Games in Brisbane in 2032.

Rugby.title: All Blacks 2IC on big shift coaches are making with 2026 squad
Rugby.summary: The new All Blacks coaching regime is looking to reverse a trend it has identified in the country's style of play, with Monday's squad selection key to that shift.
Rugby.body:
The new All Blacks coaching regime is looking to reverse a trend it has identified in the country’s style of play, with Monday’s squad selection key to that reversal. The team’s 2IC, Neil Barnes, says there will be a renewed focus on tackle evasion and on using skill sets in the All Blacks’ attack, rather than an over-dedication to winning collisions, which he says detracts from New Zealand’s traditional strengths.

Barnes joined Dave Rennie’s coaching ticket three months prior to the first All Blacks squad announcement of 2026, with the head coach winning his position, in large part, thanks to what he described as a clear vision for how he wants the team to play. That clarity gave selectors – Rennie, Barnes, and Sir Graham Henry – the bulk of the Super Rugby Pacific season to find the best players to bring that vision to life.

Rugby.title: Neil Barnes on tightest All Blacks selections: 'You don't do s**t like that lightly'
Rugby.summary: All Blacks lead assistant coach Neil Barnes has shared some insights into the tight selection calls in the first All Blacks squad of 2026, addressing the established Test players who have been left out.
Rugby.body:
All Blacks lead assistant coach Neil Barnes has shared some insights into the tight selection calls in the first All Blacks squad of 2026, addressing the established Test players who have been left out.

Some of the names fans were most shocked not to see in Monday’s squad included Rieko Ioane and Du’Plessis Kirifi, both in their prime playing years and were All Blacks in 2025.

Rugby.title: '10 games off the bench': Ex-All Black's theory on Kyle Preston selection
Rugby.summary: One of the most surprising names in Dave Rennie's first All Black squad was not a debutant, but second year halfback Kyle Preston from the Crusaders.
Rugby.body:
One of the most surprising names in Dave Rennie’s first All Black squad was not a debutant, but second year halfback Kyle Preston from the Crusaders. The three halfbacks who made the cut were Hurricanes star Cam Roigard, Chiefs’ Cortez Ratima and Kyle Preston.

Preston beat out a handful of capped All Black No.9s, including his club teammate at the Crusaders Noah Hotham who had started all year ahead of him in Christchurch. Blues halfback Finlay Christie, with 28-Test caps, also missed out. Christie started the Eden Park Test against the Springboks last year, which was Preston’s All Black debut where he had a handful of minutes off the bench.

Rugby.title: 'I'm very content': Crusaders capatin David Havili announces departure
Rugby.summary: David Havili's time in red and black has come to an end after one of the most successful Super Rugby careers in the competition's history.
Rugby.body:
David Havili’s time in red and black has come to an end after one of the most successful Super Rugby careers in the competition’s history. Departing the club after two seasons as captain, Havili was involved in all eight of the Crusaders’ recent championship seasons, starting at fullback in four finals and second five-eighth in three, while missing the 2023 final through an injury suffered just ahead of the playoffs.

Having debuted for the club in 2015, the 31-year-old has 155 Crusaders caps to his name and was just the eighth player, and first back, to be named Crusaders captain, following in the footsteps of Richard Loe, Todd Blackadder, Reuben Thorne, Richie McCaw, Kieran Read, Sam Whitelock, and Scott Barrett.

Rugby.title: Mike Blair on Kirifi: 'There's some pretty impressive players in front of him'
Rugby.summary: Both players had exceptional seasons, but Rennie and his coaching staff opted for the German-born Blues No.7, over the eight Test All Black.
Rugby.body:
While Dave Rennie had the opportunity to give Anton Segner a maiden All Blacks call-up, there was also a difficult conversation to be had with Hurricanes captain Du’Plessis Kirifi.

Segner, one of the four uncapped players to be named in Monday’s 34-man squad, was likely battling Kirifi for one spot in Rennie’s loose-forward mix. Despite Kirifi co-captaining his side to a Super Rugby Pacific title, assistant coach Mike Blair said that the loose-forward position was tough to pick, and there are a number of players who missed out.

Rugby.title: Veteran Black Ferns lock signs fresh extension before Lions Tour
Rugby.summary: "It’s great to see our women’s players making long term commitments to the game as we look forward to an exciting international calendar over the next three years.”
Rugby.body:
Veteran Black Ferns lock Maiakawanakaulani Roos is sticking around for the next two years, penning an extension with New Zealand Rugby ahead of the historic British and Irish Lions Tour on Kiwi shores.

The 41-Test Black Fern has been a staple for the national side since making her debut back in 2021, scoring three tries in her international career to date. Roos said signing an extension was important to her, as the Black Ferns and Blues environments provided her with an opportunity to begin her career.
